note: Google Programmable Search (google_cse) embeds Google's hosted Programmable Search Engine (CSE) into Drupal via a core Search page plugin 'google_cse_search' and a 'google_cse' block. IMPORTANT: there is NO google_cse.settings config object — the plugin's settings (cx, results_display, custom_results_display, query_key, watermark, prefix/suffix, width, custom_css, data_attributes) are stored INSIDE a search.page.<id> config entity under configuration.* (schema search.plugin.google_cse_search), created via core's Search pages UI (/admin/config/search/pages); the module has no configure route of its own. LIMITATION: returning live results requires a real Google Programmable Search Engine (a valid cx) and the visitor's browser running Google's script, which this environment does not have — so medium/hard cases are grounded in CONFIG ONLY (create/read the search.page entity's configuration.* keys), never in live search results. Easy cases are response-graded (no site changes). Medium cases stage a known google_cse search page and ask the agent to read its config back (cleanup deletes it). Hard cases have the agent create + configure a google_cse search page with a dummy cx and on-site options, verified against live config (exit 0 = pass).

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with the Google Programmable Search (google_cse) module, how do you expose a Google-powered search? Name the core search plugin it provides and where in the admin UI you create an instance of it.
evals.expected_output: It provides a core Search page plugin with id google_cse_search ('Google Programmable Search'). Create an instance at /admin/config/search/pages ('Add search page', choose the Google Programmable Search plugin) — it becomes a search.page config entity. No Search API/Solr backend is involved; Google hosts the index.

evals.prompt: Where is the Google Search Engine ID (the 'cx') stored for a google_cse search page, and what config key holds it? Is there a google_cse.settings object?
evals.expected_output: There is NO google_cse.settings object. The Search Engine ID is the configuration.cx key inside the search.page.<id> config entity whose plugin is google_cse_search (config schema search.plugin.google_cse_search). Read it with: drush cget search.page.<id> configuration.

evals.prompt: A google_cse search page has a 'results_display' option. What are its two possible values and what does each do?
evals.expected_output: results_display: 'here' renders the search results on this site via Google's embedded JavaScript element (default); 'google' redirects the search box to Google's hosted results page at https://cse.google.com/cse (the cx is sent as a hidden field). It is a configuration.* key on the search.page entity.

evals.prompt: Which permission does google_cse define to control who can run Google Programmable Search, and which permission controls editing the search configuration?
evals.expected_output: The module defines the 'search Google CSE' permission (label 'View Google Programmable Search') to gate running searches. Editing the search page configuration is gated by Drupal core's 'administer search' permission.

evals.prompt: This Drupal 11 site has a Google Programmable Search page configured. Inspect the live configuration and report: what is its Google Search Engine ID (cx), and are its results shown on this site or redirected to Google?
evals.expected_output: The search.page.google_eval_known entity uses the google_cse_search plugin with configuration.cx = eval-known-778899:sampleengine and configuration.results_display = google (search box redirects to Google's hosted results page).

evals.prompt: On this live Drupal 11 site, a Google Programmable Search page is set up with non-default display options. Inspect its configuration and report: which results layout (custom_results_display) is selected, is the Google watermark shown, and which query-string key carries the search terms?
evals.expected_output: search.page.google_eval_layout: configuration.custom_results_display = two-column, configuration.watermark = true (watermark shown), configuration.query_key = q. Read via drush cget search.page.google_eval_layout configuration.

evals.prompt: Set up Google-powered search on this site. Use my Google search engine id 'eval-demo-000111:dummyengine'. I want the search results to appear on our own site (not send visitors off to Google), using the plain results-only layout. (This is a test environment with no real Google engine, so I only need it configured correctly — it won't return live results yet.)
evals.expected_output: A search.page config entity using the google_cse_search plugin exists with configuration.cx = eval-demo-000111:dummyengine, configuration.results_display = here, and configuration.custom_results_display = results-only.

evals.prompt: Create a core Search page that uses the google_cse (Google Programmable Search) plugin. Set configuration.cx to 'eval-demo-000111:dummyengine', configuration.results_display to 'here', and configuration.custom_results_display to 'results-only'. It does not need to reach a real Google engine — the search.page config entity just needs to exist and be correctly configured.
evals.expected_output: A search.page config entity with plugin google_cse_search exists whose configuration.cx == eval-demo-000111:dummyengine, results_display == here, custom_results_display == results-only.
